I'd just finished splitting up the scrap two-by-fours into kindling

Working the lowlands door-to-door, like a Latter-Day Saint
And then you turned me out at the top of the stair
You took all the glory that you just couldn't share

I'm out of the game
If you are the healer
It means I'm broken and lame
If thine is the glory then
Mine must be the shame
You want it darker
We kill the flame

glory.linePrev: Me and mother hubbard Papa one-eyed Jack
glory.line: My eyes have seen the glory of the draining of the ditch
glory.lineNext: I only come to Baton Rouge I gotta find myself a witch

I only come to Baton Rouge I gotta find myself a witch
I'm gonna snatch me up a couple of em every time it rains
You'll see a locomotive probably thinking its a train

glory.linePrev: He was playing on the sidewalk for passing change
glory.line: When something strange happened, glory train passed through him
glory.lineNext: So he buried the coins he made in People's Park
stanza: He was playing on the sidewalk for passing change
When something strange happened, glory train passed through him
So he buried the coins he made in People's Park
And went looking for a woman to court and spark

glory.linePrev: I saw her wince, I saw her cry
glory.line: I saw the glory in her eye
glory.lineNext: Myself, I long for love and light
stanza: I saw her wince, I saw her cry
I saw the glory in her eye
Myself, I long for love and light
But must it come so cruel, and oh so bright?

gracefully.linePrev: With silver bracelets on her wrists and flowers in her hair
gracefully.line: She walked up to me so gracefully and took my crown of thorns
gracefully.lineNext: "Come in," she said, "I'll give ya shelter from the storm"
stanza: Suddenly I turned around and she was standing there
With silver bracelets on her wrists and flowers in her hair
She walked up to me so gracefully and took my crown of thorns
"Come in," she said, "I'll give ya shelter from the storm"

gracefully.linePrev: Excitement is ecstatic, passion places bets
gracefully.line: Gracefully he bows to ovations that he gets
gracefully.lineNext: But the hands that are applauding are slippery with sweat
stanza: The Spanish bulls are beaten, the crowd is soon beguiled
The matador is beautiful, a symphony of style
Excitement is ecstatic, passion places bets
Gracefully he bows to ovations that he gets
But the hands that are applauding are slippery with sweat
And saliva is falling from their smiles

gamble.linePrev: Shows there's money to be made
gamble.lineNext: That's the history of the trade
stanza: Picking up the ticket
Shows there's money to be made
Go on, lose the gamble
That's the history of the trade
Did you add up all the cards
Left to play to zero

gamble.linePrev: And sweet heaven when I die
gamble.line: Now I've tried drinking rye and gambling
gamble.lineNext: Dancing with damnation is a ball
stanza: Now I've tried drinking rye and gambling
Dancing with damnation is a ball
But of all the little ways I've found to hurt myself
Well you might be my favorite one of all

gamble.linePrev: She was with the Russians too?
stanza: I was gambling in Havana
I took a little risk
Send lawyers, guns, and money
Dad, get me out of this
Ha!

gamble.line: The sun's upon a gambling day
gamble.lineNext: His Queen smiled low and blissfully
stanza: The sun's upon a gambling day
His Queen smiled low and blissfully
Let's make some wretched fool to pay
Plain it was, she did agree

gamble.linePrev: "Come in," she said, "I'll give ya shelter from the storm"
gamble.line: In a little hilltop village, they gambled for my clothes
gamble.lineNext: I bargained for salvation, and she give me a lethal dose
stanza: In a little hilltop village, they gambled for my clothes
I bargained for salvation, and she give me a lethal dose
I offered up my innocence and I got repaid with scorn
"Come in," she said, "I'll give ya shelter from the storm"

gamble.line: Gambling with the odds of fate

Gambling with the odds of fate
Tryin' to get over
Tryin' to get over
Tryin' to get over
Tryin' to get over

gamble.line: Gambling the tiny shards of brass

And then my eyesight
All together playing games of cards
Gambling the tiny shards of brass
Once my heart

goin.linePrev: I'm sure your thoughts are not with me
goin.line: But with the country to where you're goin'
goin.lineNext: So, take heed, take heed of the western winds
stanza: If a-you, my love, must think that-a-way
I'm sure your mind is a-roamin'
I'm sure your thoughts are not with me
But with the country to where you're goin'

goin.linePrev: 'Til someone needs medical help or the magnolias bloom
goin.line: It's cold in this house and I ain't goin' out to chop wood
goin.lineNext: So cover me up and know you're enough to use me for good
stanza: So, girl, leave your boots by the bed, we ain't leavin' this room
'Til someone needs medical help or the magnolias bloom
It's cold in this house and I ain't goin' out to chop wood
So cover me up and know you're enough to use me for good

goin.linePrev: I got women up and down this creek
goin.line: And they keep me goin' and my engine clean
goin.lineNext: Run me ragged but I don't fret
stanza: Early in the mornin' when the sun does rise
Layin' in the bed with bloodshot eyes
Late in the evenin' when the sun sinks low
That's about time my rooster crows
I got women up and down this creek
And they keep me goin' and my engine clean
Run me ragged but I don't fret
'Cause there ain't been one slow me down, none yet

goin.linePrev: Seeing his reflection in the lives of all the lonely men
goin.line: Who reach for anything they can to keep from goin' home
goin.lineNext: Standin' in the corner, Casey drinks his pint of bitter
stanza: Casey leaves the underground and stops inside the Golden Crown
For something wet to wipe away the chill that's on his bone
Seeing his reflection in the lives of all the lonely men
Who reach for anything they can to keep from goin' home

goin.linePrev: Darkest of night, with the moon shining bright
goin.line: There's a set goin' strong; lotta things goin' on
goin.lineNext: The man of the hour has an air of great power
stanza: Darkest of night, with the moon shining bright
There's a set goin' strong; lotta things goin' on
The man of the hour has an air of great power
The dudes have envied him for so long

goin.linePrev: From the rockin' of the cradle to the rollin' of the hearse
goin.line: The goin' up was worth the comin' down
goin.lineNext: He's a poet and he's a picker, he's a prophet and he's a pusher
stanza: He has tasted good and evil in your bedrooms and your bars
And he's traded in tomorrow for today
Runnin' from his devils, Lord, and reachin' for the stars
And losin' all he's loved along the way
But if this world keeps right on turnin' for the better or the worse
And all he ever gets is older and around
From the rockin' of the cradle to the rollin' of the hearse
The goin' up was worth the comin' down

goin.linePrev: Never knowin' if believin' is a blessin' or a curse
goin.line: Or if the goin' up was worth the comin' down
goin.lineNext: He's a poet and he's a picker, he's a prophet and he's a pusher
stanza: See him wasted on the sidewalk in his jacket and his jeans
Wearin' yesterday's misfortunes like a smile
Once he had a future full of money, love, and dreams
Which he spent like they was goin' outta style
And he keeps right on a-changin' for the better or the worse
Searchin' for a shrine, he's never found
Never knowin' if believin' is a blessin' or a curse
Or if the goin' up was worth the comin' down

goin.lineNext: They got grubby little fingers and dirty little minds

Short people got nobody

You got to pick 'em up just to say hello
They got little cars that go beep, beep, beep
They got little voices goin' peep, peep, peep
They got grubby little fingers and dirty little minds
They're gonna get you every time

stanza: I elope, in these dark thoughts of a man
Reaching for the ends to which they span
I'm creeping on myself I just need the white van
Weighing actions versus consequences by the gram
I've been blowing myself up like '42 japan
I'm the number one rank in any clan
And I'm just speaking frank, Anne
And I'm walking on the plank of the shark tank
My boat already sank so I'd better start to thank
Oil the gears as they crank, swivel the gun on the tank
And I'm the dope boy and this here is dank
Every song has a ploy, I come out with a shank
This ain't a toy, this the gun of a tank
This ain't a coin, this is the whole bank
I'm the dope boy, my product is dank

gear.line: I'm in a high gear transmission and I just sparked the ignition
gear.lineNext: I been creeping through the peep hole
stanza: I'm on sixteenth alley in the Cascade valley
North of Cali', I been working out my mind like Bally
Cooking something up on the galley
Putting beats in body bags, add up the tally
My raps are pieces of art, I got a whole gallery
I can pep talk a rally into a riot for the finale
Flow sweeter than berries like Halle
Ooh, I like them caramel girls
The scandalous girls, fuck me like an animal girls
Bring out my instincts, make me go primate
Annihilate your eardrums with vibrate
Tions, I hydrate with nitrate, so I flow with nitrous, let loose like
On Friday night with a pipe
Packed with kief of the leaves
That allows me to catch Zs
'Cause I don't sleep, Geez Louise
Nicky G, He's the missing piece of the puzzle
Between peace versus muzzles, I paint a picture with my easel
I got a lot of power I need diesel
With a flow so lethal my language needs to be illegal
I got the nose of a beagle and the talons of an eagle
I turn ya into fecal then roll you around like a dung beetle
I'll stab you with a bees needle and put you in the fetal
Position, a fucking animal on every composition
159 is the addition of my cognition
So when I'm speaking you need to shut the fuck up and listen
Just kidding, but I'm just more than a musician
I got food for thought that's nutrition
I'm a poetic dietician and fuck paying tuition
I'd rather spray my ammunition
I'm in a high gear transmission and I just sparked the ignition

grandmamas.linePrev: Before he sent down his angels for you?
grandmamas.lineNext: 'Cause that's all they know how to do
stanza: Mamas and grandmamas love you
'Cause that's all they know how to do
You never planned on the bombs in the sand
Or sleeping in your dress blues

gymnasium's.linePrev: Or sleeping in your dress blues
gymnasium's.line: Now the high school gymnasium's ready

stanza: Now the high school gymnasium's ready
Full of flowers and old legionnaires
Nobody showed up to protest
Just sniffle and stare

goddamn.linePrev: Who wouldn't down there, wouldn't you?
stanza: Her own mother came to meet us at the station
Her dress as black as a crow in a coal mine
She cried when her little girl got off the train
Her brothers and her sisters came down from Jackson, Mississippi
In a great green Hudson driven by a Gentile they knew
Drinkin' rye whiskey from a flask in the backseat
Tryin' to do like the Gentiles do
Christ, they wanted to be Gentiles, too
Who wouldn't down there, wouldn't you?
An American Christian, goddamn!

goddamn.songTitle: hope is a dangerous thing for a woman like me to have - but i have it
goddamn.linePrev: I've been tearing up town in my fucking white gown
goddamn.lineNext: Shaking my ass is the only thing that's
stanza: I've been tearing up town in my fucking white gown
Like a goddamn near sociopath
Shaking my ass is the only thing that's
Got this black narcissist off my back
She couldn't care less, and I never cared more
So there's no more to say about that
Except hope is a dangerous thing for a woman like me to have
Hope is a dangerous thing for a woman with my past

goddamn.linePrev: I don't need to change my strings 'cause the dirt don't hurt the way I sing
goddamn.line: I don't have to do a Goddamn thing except sit around and wait to die
goddamn.lineNext: Well, I've been waiting on an angel waitress to come and take my order
stanza: Ain't no point getting outta bed, if you ain't living the dream
It's like making a big old pot of coffee when you ain't got no cream
I don't need to change my strings 'cause the dirt don't hurt the way I sing
I don't have to do a Goddamn thing except sit around and wait to die

goddamn.linePrev: I know system, I know poison in the beer
goddamn.line: L God whisperin' in my goddamn ear
stanza: L God whisperin' in my goddamn ear
Temptations surround
Head swivelin', Question pivotin'
Brought to you by my pop's influence

goddamn.linePrev: I'm out here living in a fantasy
goddamn.line: I can't enjoy a goddamn thing
stanza: Anxiety
How do you always get the best of me?
I'm out here living in a fantasy
I can't enjoy a goddamn thing
Anxiety
Why am I never where I am supposed to be?
Even with my lover sleeping close to me
I'm wide awake and I'm in pain

goddamn.linePrev: And if she didn't believe, she didn't dare give me slack
goddamn.line: Or It was "baby I love you, get off of my goddamn back"
goddamn.lineNext: Time went by, and I left, and I left again
stanza: Had a girl back home, and we shared a single bed
When I whispered in her ear, she believed every word I said
And if she didn't believe, she didn't dare give me slack
Or It was "baby I love you, get off of my goddamn back"
Time went by, and I left, and I left again
Jesus loves a sinner, but the highway loves a sin
My daddy told me, I believe he told me true
That the right thing's always the hardest thing to do

galaxy.linePrev: And the terror of the idea of fatherhood at twenty-three destroyed my foundatio…
galaxy.line: Though my life is a galaxy of subtleties
galaxy.lineNext: My complex intentions and aspirations do not matter at all

My complex intentions and aspirations do not matter at all
In the face of the crushing flow of actual time

That my descendants will squint back through a fog trying to see
Some polluted version of all I meant to be in life
Their recollections pruned by the accidents of time, what got thrown away, and…

galaxy.linePrev: My mind is taking time for some stretchin'
galaxy.line: Bending galaxies into my verbal collection
galaxy.lineNext: My ethics remain the most pristine

The vegetables of rhyme, nutrition for your mind
Poke your nerves in your spine

Spit the most venomous, leave you stranded in a nebulous
Unless you're in for my best interest
I'll show you benevolence, fly us away on a Pegasus
Let you benefit from my intelligence
My rhymes are the perception of medicine that is perfection
Known to cure cancer, bulimia and depression
That last one is a self-tested assessment

glimmer.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
glimmer.linePrev: On the meadow, the hill and the shore
glimmer.line: They sing no more by the glimmer of the moon
glimmer.lineNext: On the bench by the old cabin door
stanza: They hunt no more for the 'possum and the coon
On the meadow, the hill and the shore
They sing no more by the glimmer of the moon
On the bench by the old cabin door
The day goes by like a shadow o'er the heart
With sorrow where all was delight
The time has come when the darkies have to part
Then my old Kentucky home, good night!

glimmer.linePrev: Her hair of floating sky is shimmering
stanza: 2 ContributorsJulia LyricsHalf of what I say is meaningless
But I say it just to reach you, Julia
Julia, Julia
Ocean child calls me
So I sing the song of love, Julia
Julia
Seashell eyes, windy smile calls me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Her hair of floating sky is shimmering
Glimmering in the sun
Julia, Julia
Morning moon touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
When I cannot sing my heart
I can only speak my mind
Julia
Julia
Sleeping sand, silent cloud touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Calls me
So I sing the song of love for Julia
Julia, Julia

grill.linePrev: The wind was blowing especially through her hair

Trying to save our marriage and perhaps catch a few fish

Humming the tune to 'Louie Louie'
Aah baby, we gotta go now

Oh tick tock goes the pendulum
On the old grandfather clock

graceful.linePrev: If your life is a leaf that the seasons tear off and condemn
graceful.line: They will bind you with love that is graceful and green as a stem
graceful.lineNext: When I left, they were sleeping. I hope you run into them soon
stanza: Well they lay down beside me, I made my confession to them
They touched both my eyes and I touched the dew on their hem
If your life is a leaf that the seasons tear off and condemn
They will bind you with love that is graceful and green as a stem
Graceful sleeper
You midnight dreamer
I'm floored by your sound
I'm floored by your sound

gyp.linePrev: And root to root and tip to tip
gyp.line: I look at him, my country gyp
gyp.lineNext: Leathered up by all his fears
stanza: And root to root and tip to tip
I look at him, my country gyp
Leathered up by all his fears
But someone brought you close to tears

partner.line: And root to root and tip to tip

stanza: And I'm sorry to whichever man
Should meet my sorry state
Watch my sturdy, lonesome gait
And beware

partner.line: Should meet my sorry state

geneva.line: He wants to die in a lake in Geneva
geneva.lineNext: The mountains can cover the shape of his nose
stanza: He wants to die in a lake in Geneva
The mountains can cover the shape of his nose
He wants to die where nobody can see him
But the beauty of his death will carry on, so
I don't believe him
*Camp Cope - How to Socialise & Make Friends
*Skepta - Konnichiwa
Grimes - Art Angels

garbage.line: Been saved again by the garbage truck
garbage.lineNext: I got something to say, you know but nothing comes
stanza: Excuse me, but can I be you for a while?
My dog won't bite if you sit real still
I got the Antichrist in the kitchen yelling at me again
Yeah, I can hear that
Been saved again by the garbage truck
I got something to say, you know but nothing comes
Yes I know what you think of me, you never shut up
Yeah, I can hear that

garbage.linePrev: And she shows you where to look
garbage.line: Among the garbage and the flowers
garbage.lineNext: There are heroes in the seaweed
stanza: Now Suzanne takes your hand
And she leads you to the river
She is wearing rags and feathers
From Salvation Army counters
And the sun pours down like honey
On our lady of the harbour
And she shows you where to look
Among the garbage and the flowers
There are heroes in the seaweed
There are children in the morning
They are leaning out for love
And they will lean that way forever
While Suzanne holds the mirror

garbage.songTitle: The King of Carrot Flowers, Pt. One
garbage.linePrev: And your mom would stick a fork right into daddy's shoulder
garbage.line: And dad would throw the garbage all across the floor
garbage.lineNext: As we would lay and learn what each other's bodies were for
stanza: When you were young, you were the king of carrot flowers
And how you built a tower tumbling through the trees
In holy rattlesnakes that fell all around your feet
And your mom would stick a fork right into daddy's shoulder
And dad would throw the garbage all across the floor
As we would lay and learn what each other's bodies were for

garbage.line: I finally took out the upstairs bathroom garbage that was sitting there forgott…
garbage.lineNext: Wanting just to stay with us

glade.songTitle: Jeanie with the Light Brown Hair
glade.linePrev: I sigh for Jeanie, but her light form strayed
glade.line: Far from the fond hearts round her native glade;
glade.lineNext: Her smiles have vanished and her sweet songs flown
stanza: I sigh for Jeanie, but her light form strayed
Far from the fond hearts round her native glade;
Her smiles have vanished and her sweet songs flown
Flitting like the dreams that have cheered us and gone
Now the nodding wild flowers may wither on the shore
While her gentle fingers will cull them no more:
Oh! I sigh for Jeanie with the light brown hair
Floating, like a vapor, on the soft summer air
